original_hypothesis.title: Permanent Trapping of Curator Funds via Unbounded Protocol Fees
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The protocol owner can set the protocol fee percentage to an arbitrarily high value (>100%). This causes the sell function to revert due to arithmetic underflow, permanently trapping all curator shares.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Administrative fee setters must have hardcoded upper bounds to prevent accidental or malicious bricking of core economic functions.
preconditions: Admin key is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Missing bounds check on critical economic parameter

operational_questions: Is the admin key secured by a multisig or timelock?
Can an accidental fat-finger input brick the protocol?
validation_requirements: Test setting fee to 10001 and observe sellShareCred revert
Review deployment scripts for admin key security

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker compromises the protocol owner key
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Transaction reverts due to underflow in price - protocolFee - creatorFee

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium - requires admin to reset the fee

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
